note: Machine-readable deficiency record (BROWNFIELD-PROTOCOL v0.1 §3) — the mechanization of AUDIT.md. Human view: DEBT.md, generated from this file. Severity scale is PROP-013's (P1 blocker / P2 debt / P3 note); dispositions are fixed | filed | accepted | open.
kind_extensions: `coverage-gap` and `external-drift` extend the BROWNFIELD §3 kind set (failing-test | unimplemented-req | disputed-spec | orphan-code | stale-doc), which has no bucket for untested-production-paths or external-state drift. Recorded as v0.2 package feedback in BASELINE.md.

entries.title: Production git-registry + naming path is under-tested
entries.detail: Install e2e drive LocalRegistry (`--registry <dir>`) almost exclusively; that path bypasses GitPackageRegistry::resolve (clone / ls-remote / archive / tags) and NamingConvention::repo_name — the code a real registry install runs. The `vibe init` default-config path (no --registry) has no e2e at all. This is the gap that let the M1.19 `vibe init` defect ship green through eight phases.
evidence: crates/vibe-cli/tests/cli_e2e.rs (LocalRegistry-driven suite; `install_from_git_registry` is the lone partial precedent)
entries.sunset: re-disposition at the first full PROP-013 audit run (INT-0001)

entries.title: cli_live_e2e is #[ignore]d and red
entries.detail: Three live e2e tests against real GitHub / GitVerse are quarantined (not in the gate) and currently red against the partially-migrated test orgs. A quarantined-and-red test is neither a safety net nor a signal. Coupled to DBT-0005: the fixtures and the tests' expectations move together.
entries.sunset: re-disposition at the first full PROP-013 audit run

entries.title: fixtures/manual-test-packages/ carries retired schema
entries.detail: flow-vibevm-github-smoke (and likely flow-vibevm-direct-push-smoke) still use [writes] and [boot_snippet].filename and carry no [package].group — all retired by M1.18 and PROP-008. No hermetic test parses these fixtures, so the gate stays green while they rot. De-rot or delete if the manual-test recipes no longer reference them.
entries.sunset: re-disposition at the first full PROP-013 audit run

entries.title: GitVerse registry side un-migrated to fqdn
entries.detail: vibespecs-gitverse and the vibespecstest3 GitVerse org still predate PROP-008. The GitHub publish token does not apply to GitVerse and GitVerse exposes no API DELETE — owner web-UI / owner-token work. Gates nothing in-repo.
entries.sunset: re-disposition at the first full PROP-013 audit run

entries.title: GitHub test orgs vibespecstest1/2 un-migrated
entries.detail: Both orgs still carry kind-name-shaped fixture repos. Re-laying them out is coupled to the #[ignore]d cli_live_e2e tests (DBT-0002) — the fixtures and the tests' expectations are one unit of work.
entries.sunset: re-disposition at the first full PROP-013 audit run

entries.title: Legacy vibespecs/flow-* repos archived, not deleted
entries.detail: The M1.19 migration archived flow-wal / flow-sync-from-code / flow-atomic-commits (read-only, reversible) rather than deleting. Accepted: archive is the reversible tidy; the owner can delete outright if a fully-clean org is wanted.
entries.accepted_reason: reversible tidy; deletion stays available to the owner
entries.sunset: re-judge at the first full PROP-013 audit run

entries.title: PROP-005 references a non-existent schemas/ directory
entries.detail: PROP-005 §2.6 / §3.1 cite crates/vibe-index/schemas/index-entry.jtd.json, but no schemas/ directory exists under crates/vibe-index/ — the index wire types are hand-rolled serde structs. Reconcile the PROP to the implementation, or add the JTD schemas it describes.
entries.sunset: re-disposition at the first full PROP-013 audit run

entries.title: Residual doc requalification deferred from PROP-008 Phase 8
entries.detail: Phase 8 reconciled the identity-defining docs but deliberately left a cosmetic sweep — requalifying (kind, name) / <kind>:<name> example forms across ~15 peripheral command docs.
entries.sunset: re-disposition at the first full PROP-013 audit run

entries.title: Deferred PROP-011 refinements
entries.detail: Two recorded deferrals: a content_hash slot spot-check for slot_integrity = verify (needs compute_content_hash lowered out of vibe-registry), and true incremental re-resolution that skips the registry walk for an unchanged subtree (needs PROP-003's SAT pin_preferences). Both gate on other work.
entries.sunset: re-disposition at the first full PROP-013 audit run

entries.title: Parked backlog: workspace version inheritance; publish-signalling polish
entries.detail: version = { workspace = true } member-version inheritance (PROP-007 §6 q4) and the publish-signalling polish (--archive, has_issues) were parked behind larger milestones. Re-judge whether either is still wanted.
entries.sunset: re-disposition at the first full PROP-013 audit run

entries.title: NaiveDepSolver is still the only depsolver
entries.detail: PROP-003's SAT solver (resolvo / libsolv) is unimplemented; NaiveDepSolver (DFS, no backtracking) handles the current scale. Several deferred items (DBT-0009) gate on the SAT solver. Architectural ceiling, not urgent at current package counts.
entries.adjudication: the backtracking half retires: crates/vibe-resolver/src/sat.rs implements chronological backtracking over version choices, reusing NaiveDepSolver as the branch checker (so feature/conditional/capability semantics cannot drift between the cells), pinned by the dominance differential in tests/solver_properties.rs (sat solves identically where naive solves; solves strictly more where naive's first-pick-wins traps — proven by the property suite finding such a world on its first run) plus four unit cases. The industrial-solver half (resolvo primary per PROP-002 §2.8) stays an owner option, recorded as the deviates edge on Sat's DepSolver impl; production solver selection (registry flag, R-001) is the remaining wiring, tracked by the cell sweep.
entries.sunset: met — adopt-v0.3 Phase 7; resolvo adoption remains owner-gated

entries.title: PROP-002 §2.5 vs PROP-008 §2.5 — naming-convention default and value set
entries.detail: PROP-002 still declares kind-name the default repo-naming convention and lists a value set without fqdn; PROP-008 (SHIPPED 2026-05-22) titles fqdn as the convention, ships NamingConvention::Fqdn as the code default, and the canonical org is migrated to that shape. A reader implementing against PROP-002 today builds the wrong model. Recorded, not resolved — adjudication is the owner's act (BROWNFIELD §5: supersede / scope-split / stay-open).
evidence: PROP-002:63 — "`naming` — convention for mapping a pkgref to a package repo name under this org. Values: `\"kind-name\"` (default — `flow:wal` → `<org>/flow-wal`), `\"name\"` (...), `\"kind/name\"` (...)"
PROP-008:80 — "### 2.5 Repository naming — `naming = \"fqdn\"`"; PROP-008:88 — "naming = \"fqdn\"  # repo name = \"<group>.<name>\"  →  org.vibevm.wal"
fix cc32d7e made `vibe init` / `vibe registry add` scaffold fqdn (the shipped default)
entries.adjudication: supersede — PROP-008 wins (owner sanction 2026-06-10); PROP-002 §2.2 now leads with fqdn and keeps kind-name documented as legacy
entries.sunset: re-verify at the first full PROP-013 audit run

entries.title: spec/boot/00-core.md vs spec/boot/90-user.md — where the package registry lives
entries.detail: 00-core.md names GitVerse as the (future) package-registry home; 90-user.md records the deliberate split-host posture — registry on GitHub, only vibevm source on GitVerse — and all v0.1.0+ publishing/consumption goes through GitHub (live since 2026-04-29). Both files are frozen surfaces (owner-owned; PLAYBOOK §0.4), so even the trivial fix is owner-only. Recorded, not resolved.
evidence: 90-user.md:14 — "Package registry — GitHub organization `vibespecs`: `https://github.com/vibespecs`. (...) All publishing and consumption of v0.1.0+ packages goes through GitHub."
entries.adjudication: supersede — 90-user/split-host reality wins; 00-core.md edited under the owner's explicit in-session sanction for frozen surfaces (2026-06-10)
entries.sunset: re-verify at the first full PROP-013 audit run

entries.title: spec/boot/90-user.md repo-shape line vs PROP-008 / live org reality
entries.detail: 90-user.md still describes per-package repos as <kind>-<name> under NamingConvention::KindName "per PROP-002 §2.5"; PROP-008 shipped fqdn as the default and the canonical org was migrated 2026-05-22 to org.vibevm.* repos (legacy flow-* archived). Frozen surface — owner-only edit. Linked to DBT-0012 (same subject, different unit pair).
evidence: WAL 2026-05-23 — the canonical GitHub org hosts org.vibevm.{wal,sync-from-code,atomic-commits}; flow-* archived
entries.adjudication: supersede — PROP-008/live-org reality wins; 90-user.md edited under the owner's explicit in-session sanction for frozen surfaces (2026-06-10)
entries.sunset: re-verify at the first full PROP-013 audit run

entries.title: PROP-003 carries a duplicate {#phases} anchor — spec:// URI ambiguity
entries.detail: Two sections of PROP-003 declare the same anchor, so spec://vibevm/modules/vibe-resolver/PROP-003#phases resolves ambiguously. Found by the Phase −1 heuristic scan (the only same-file duplicate in spec/**). Directly relevant: PROP-003 §2.6.1 is the Phase 1 pilot target, and Phase 1 adds anchored req units to this file — the anchor space should be adjudicated first. Note: not a semantic dispute between two claims; recorded under disputed-spec as an anchor-identity collision (both units claim the same address).
entries.adjudication: §6 staging plan keeps {#phases} (house convention); §3.2 renamed to {#solver-phases}; zero references existed to either meaning, verified by repo-wide grep
links: INT-0006 (vibe check deferred check #4 — anchor uniqueness — would catch this class mechanically)
entries.sunset: re-verify at the first full PROP-013 audit run

entries.title: PLAYBOOK v0.2 vs BROWNFIELD §3 — where REVIEW/TODO markers are homed
entries.detail: The playbook's Phase −1 sends REVIEW markers and load-bearing TODO/FIXME into debt.json; BROWNFIELD §3 lists the same markers as intent.json harvest sources. For this run the playbook was followed (precedence §0.2: playbook > package documents on task content) — moot in practice this round, since the marker sweep found zero load-bearing instances. Recorded as package-internal contradiction for the v0.2 revision; not resolved here.
evidence: PLAYBOOK:26 — "Seed `terraform/registry/debt.json` (...): import all open AUDIT.md findings; add failing/flaky tests; add `<!-- REVIEW -->` markers and load-bearing TODO/FIXME"
BROWNFIELD:47 — "`intent.json` (+ `INTENT.md`) — the aspiration inventory, harvested from WAL \"Next\"/\"Known issues\", `TASKS.md`, ROADMAP open milestones, `<!-- REVIEW -->` markers, TODO/FIXME"
entries.adjudication: supersede — the v0.2 package dissolves the conflict's subject: PLAYBOOK-TERRAFORM-VIBEVM-v0.2 is superseded by the generalized RAID playbook (discipline-core/03-RAID-PLAYBOOK.md) + TERRAFORM-PLAN-v0.3, and BROWNFIELD relocated to spec/discipline/ unchanged; the marker-homing question is answered by the raid skeleton (the debt ledger is the raid's, the intent harvest stays BROWNFIELD §3). Sunset clause met: the v0.2 revision shipped.
entries.sunset: met — the discipline package v0.2 shipped (adopt-v0.3 Phase 0)

entries.title: ROADMAP internal staleness
entries.detail: Four instances: (a) M1.1-revision points at TASKS.md "at repo root" — the file does not exist; (b) the "M1 acceptance" checklist still shows shipped items unticked (M1.2 diff display, M1.4 show output, …); (c) M1.9 scope references the retired vibe-package.toml filename; (d) the side-quest list is partially overtaken (CHANGELOG.md exists; clippy -D warnings already in the self-check gate) and M1.17's header still reads 🚧 although the remainder was subsumed by PROP-009. One reconciliation pass over ROADMAP.md fixes all four.
evidence: ROADMAP.md:184 — "Task breakdown lives in [`TASKS.md`](TASKS.md) at repo root." (file absent)
ROADMAP.md:284-296 — M1 acceptance boxes unticked for shipped work
ROADMAP.md:390 — M1.9 "Optional `[package].describes` field in `vibe-package.toml`" (filename retired by M1.17)
entries.sunset: re-disposition at the first full PROP-013 audit run

entries.title: vibe init next-steps hint still leads with the kind-qualified pkgref shape
entries.detail: The scaffold's closing hint reads "install packages with `vibe install <kind>:<name>` (e.g. flow:wal)" — the pre-PROP-008 emphasis. The kind: prefix is still legal pkgref grammar, but the canonical qualified form (org.vibevm/wal) and the short-name sugar are what PROP-008 documents lead with. Surfaced by the Phase −1 characterization capture.
entries.sunset: re-disposition at the first full PROP-013 audit run

entries.title: vibe-core leaf modules have no scannable spec home until VIBEVM-SPEC.md is unit-ified
entries.detail: error.rs, timestamp.rs, and values.rs carry public items whose governing text lives in VIBEVM-SPEC.md (values.rs cites §5.3 verbatim) — but the owner-frozen root spec sits outside spec/** and is not scanned by mdspec, so no spec:// unit exists to tag against. The six symbols are dispositioned in specmap-ratchet.json under this id. Resolution path: either extend the scanner to the root document (an owner decision — the file is frozen) or move the governing sections into spec/ units; both are out of terraform scope.
entries.fixed_by: depth-program P1 (2026-06-12): mdspec scans VIBEVM-SPEC.md as an explicit root spec doc; 90 section anchors landed (additive only, per the owner's depth-program directive); the trio carries scope! edges into #typed-value-system / #lockfile-schema / #package-identity+#manifest-schema; the six ratchet dispositions are retired and vibe-cli left the exempt list
entries.sunset: closes when VIBEVM-SPEC §5.3 (and the error/timestamp contracts) exist as scannable units

entries.title: The MCP surface has no spec home (no module PROP, no VIBEVM-SPEC section)
entries.detail: vibe-mcp (the M1.7 JSON-RPC server crate) and vibe-cli's commands/mcp.rs (agent detection, config-file install/upgrade/uninstall, the vendored skill template) implement a surface VIBEVM-SPEC.md predates and no PROP governs. The ten top-level pub items of commands/mcp.rs are dispositioned in specmap-ratchet.json under this id; the vibe-mcp crate stays ratchet-exempt for the same reason. Resolution path: author a FEAT/PROP for the MCP surface (or an owner amendment to VIBEVM-SPEC.md), then tag both crates and retire the dispositions.
evidence: crates/vibe-cli/src/commands/mcp.rs (10 pub items, listed in specmap-ratchet.json)
entries.source: depth-program P1 (2026-06-12) — surfaced when vibe-cli entered the orphan gate
entries.sunset: closes when an MCP FEAT/PROP (or a VIBEVM-SPEC amendment) gives the surface scannable units
